# Equipment Manual — Blast Furnace Stove (BFS-01)

**Asset ID:** BFS-01  
**Plant Area:** Iron Making  
**Criticality:** Critical  
**Manufacturer:** Siemens  
**Installed:** 2019

## 1. Overview
The Blast Furnace Stove (BFS-01) is a critical-criticality asset in the Iron Making area. This manual describes its monitored parameters, normal operating envelope, common fault modes, recommended maintenance schedule, troubleshooting guidance, and associated spare parts.

## 2. Monitored Parameters & Normal Operating Envelope
| Parameter | Unit | Normal Low | Normal High |
|---|---|---|---|
| dome_temperature | degC | 1100 | 1450 |
| waste_gas_temp | degC | 250 | 400 |
| combustion_air_flow | Nm3/h | 40000 | 60000 |
| shell_temperature | degC | 60 | 120 |

Operation outside these limits should trigger investigation. Sustained excursions indicate developing faults and should be escalated per the alerting matrix.

## 3. Common Fault Modes
### 3.1 Checker brick degradation
Thermal cycling fatigue and dust deposition reduce heat-transfer efficiency.

**Typical root causes:** Excessive thermal cycling, Poor gas cleaning upstream, Overdue checker inspection.

### 3.2 Hot blast valve leakage
Worn valve seat allows hot blast to bypass, lowering blast temperature.

**Typical root causes:** Seat erosion, Inadequate cooling water, Refractory spalling.

### 3.3 Shell hot-spot
Localised refractory loss raises shell temperature with risk of breakthrough.

**Typical root causes:** Refractory wear, Cooling stave blockage, Scaffold formation.

## 4. Recommended Maintenance Schedule
| Task | Interval |
|---|---|
| Visual inspection & cleaning | Weekly |
| Lubrication / fluid check | Monthly |
| Vibration & thermography survey | Quarterly |
| Condition-based overhaul | As indicated by trend / RUL |

## 5. Troubleshooting Guide
- **Symptom related to Checker brick degradation:** Check Excessive thermal cycling, Poor gas cleaning upstream. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.
- **Symptom related to Hot blast valve leakage:** Check Seat erosion, Inadequate cooling water. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.
- **Symptom related to Shell hot-spot:** Check Refractory wear, Cooling stave blockage. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.

## 6. Associated Spare Parts
- Checker bricks (silica)
- Hot blast valve seat
- Cooling stave
- Burner nozzle

## 7. Safety Notes
Always apply Lockout-Tagout (LOTO) before intervention. Confirm zero energy state. Refer to the relevant SOP before performing any maintenance task on this asset.

# Equipment Manual — BOF Lance Hoist (BOF-LH-01)

**Asset ID:** BOF-LH-01  
**Plant Area:** Steel Making  
**Criticality:** Critical  
**Manufacturer:** Siemens  
**Installed:** 2009

## 1. Overview
The BOF Lance Hoist (BOF-LH-01) is a critical-criticality asset in the Steel Making area. This manual describes its monitored parameters, normal operating envelope, common fault modes, recommended maintenance schedule, troubleshooting guidance, and associated spare parts.

## 2. Monitored Parameters & Normal Operating Envelope
| Parameter | Unit | Normal Low | Normal High |
|---|---|---|---|
| hoist_motor_current | A | 60 | 200 |
| rope_tension | kN | 20 | 80 |
| brake_temperature | degC | 30 | 75 |
| lance_cooling_flow | m3/h | 120 | 200 |

Operation outside these limits should trigger investigation. Sustained excursions indicate developing faults and should be escalated per the alerting matrix.

## 3. Common Fault Modes
### 3.1 Cooling water flow drop
Reduced lance cooling risks lance burn-through during blow.

**Typical root causes:** Pump cavitation, Strainer blockage, Valve fault, Scale build-up.

### 3.2 Wire rope degradation
Broken wires and tension irregularity threaten lance drop.

**Typical root causes:** Fatigue, Corrosion, Overload, Sheave wear.

### 3.3 Brake fade
Rising brake temperature reduces holding capacity.

**Typical root causes:** Worn brake pads, Hydraulic leak, Misadjustment.

## 4. Recommended Maintenance Schedule
| Task | Interval |
|---|---|
| Visual inspection & cleaning | Weekly |
| Lubrication / fluid check | Monthly |
| Vibration & thermography survey | Quarterly |
| Condition-based overhaul | As indicated by trend / RUL |

## 5. Troubleshooting Guide
- **Symptom related to Cooling water flow drop:** Check Pump cavitation, Strainer blockage. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.
- **Symptom related to Wire rope degradation:** Check Fatigue, Corrosion. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.
- **Symptom related to Brake fade:** Check Worn brake pads, Hydraulic leak. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.

## 6. Associated Spare Parts
- Wire rope
- Brake pad set
- Cooling water pump
- Sheave wheel

## 7. Safety Notes
Always apply Lockout-Tagout (LOTO) before intervention. Confirm zero energy state. Refer to the relevant SOP before performing any maintenance task on this asset.

# Equipment Manual — Continuous Casting Mould Oscillator (CCM-OSC-01)

**Asset ID:** CCM-OSC-01  
**Plant Area:** Continuous Casting  
**Criticality:** Critical  
**Manufacturer:** Primetals  
**Installed:** 2011

## 1. Overview
The Continuous Casting Mould Oscillator (CCM-OSC-01) is a critical-criticality asset in the Continuous Casting area. This manual describes its monitored parameters, normal operating envelope, common fault modes, recommended maintenance schedule, troubleshooting guidance, and associated spare parts.

## 2. Monitored Parameters & Normal Operating Envelope
| Parameter | Unit | Normal Low | Normal High |
|---|---|---|---|
| oscillation_frequency | cpm | 80 | 220 |
| hydraulic_pressure | bar | 90 | 160 |
| mould_level_dev | mm | -5 | 5 |
| oil_temperature | degC | 35 | 55 |

Operation outside these limits should trigger investigation. Sustained excursions indicate developing faults and should be escalated per the alerting matrix.

## 3. Common Fault Modes
### 3.1 Hydraulic pressure loss
Internal leakage reduces oscillation stroke, risking sticker breakouts.

**Typical root causes:** Seal wear, Contaminated oil, Pump degradation, Accumulator pre-charge loss.

### 3.2 Mould level instability
Level sensor drift or clogging causes oscillation control hunting.

**Typical root causes:** Sensor fouling, SEN clogging, Argon flow imbalance.

### 3.3 Servo valve sticking
Valve hysteresis degrades oscillation waveform fidelity.

**Typical root causes:** Oil contamination, Wear, Temperature drift.

## 4. Recommended Maintenance Schedule
| Task | Interval |
|---|---|
| Visual inspection & cleaning | Weekly |
| Lubrication / fluid check | Monthly |
| Vibration & thermography survey | Quarterly |
| Condition-based overhaul | As indicated by trend / RUL |

## 5. Troubleshooting Guide
- **Symptom related to Hydraulic pressure loss:** Check Seal wear, Contaminated oil. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.
- **Symptom related to Mould level instability:** Check Sensor fouling, SEN clogging. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.
- **Symptom related to Servo valve sticking:** Check Oil contamination, Wear. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.

## 6. Associated Spare Parts
- Servo valve
- Hydraulic seal kit
- Accumulator bladder
- Mould level sensor

## 7. Safety Notes
Always apply Lockout-Tagout (LOTO) before intervention. Confirm zero energy state. Refer to the relevant SOP before performing any maintenance task on this asset.

| hydraulic_pressure | bar | 90 | 160 |
| mould_level_dev | mm | -5 | 5 |
| oil_temperature | degC | 35 | 55 |

Operation outside these limits should trigger investigation. Sustained excursions indicate developing faults and should be escalated per the alerting matrix.

## 3. Common Fault Modes
### 3.1 Hydraulic pressure loss
Internal leakage reduces oscillation stroke, risking sticker breakouts.

**Typical root causes:** Seal wear, Contaminated oil, Pump degradation, Accumulator pre-charge loss.

### 3.2 Mould level instability
Level sensor drift or clogging causes oscillation control hunting.

**Typical root causes:** Sensor fouling, SEN clogging, Argon flow imbalance.

### 3.3 Servo valve sticking
Valve hysteresis degrades oscillation waveform fidelity.

**Typical root causes:** Oil contamination, Wear, Temperature drift.

## 4. Recommended Maintenance Schedule
| Task | Interval |
|---|---|
| Visual inspection & cleaning | Weekly |
| Lubrication / fluid check | Monthly |
| Vibration & thermography survey | Quarterly |
| Condition-based overhaul | As indicated by trend / RUL |

## 5. Troubleshooting Guide
- **Symptom related to Hydraulic pressure loss:** Check Seal wear, Contaminated oil. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.
- **Symptom related to Mould level instability:** Check Sensor fouling, SEN clogging. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.
- **Symptom related to Servo valve sticking:** Check Oil contamination, Wear. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.

## 6. Associated Spare Parts
- Servo valve
- Hydraulic seal kit
- Accumulator bladder
- Mould level sensor

## 7. Safety Notes
Always apply Lockout-Tagout (LOTO) before intervention. Confirm zero energy state. Refer to the relevant SOP before performing any maintenance task on this asset.

Operation outside these limits should trigger investigation. Sustained excursions indicate developing faults and should be escalated per the alerting matrix.

## 3. Common Fault Modes
### 3.1 Cavitation
Low suction head causes pressure pulsation and impeller pitting.

**Typical root causes:** Clogged suction strainer, Low sump level, Air ingress.

### 3.2 Mechanical seal leak
Seal failure causes leakage and bearing contamination.

**Typical root causes:** Seal face wear, Dry running, Misalignment.

### 3.3 Impeller wear
Reduced flow and pressure from worn impeller clearances.

**Typical root causes:** Abrasive water, Long service, Erosion.

**Asset ID:** RHF-WBD-01  
**Plant Area:** Hot Rolling  
**Criticality:** High  
**Manufacturer:** Danieli  
**Installed:** 2017

## 1. Overview
The Reheating Furnace Walking Beam Drive (RHF-WBD-01) is a high-criticality asset in the Hot Rolling area. This manual describes its monitored parameters, normal operating envelope, common fault modes, recommended maintenance schedule, troubleshooting guidance, and associated spare parts.

## 2. Monitored Parameters & Normal Operating Envelope
| Parameter | Unit | Normal Low | Normal High |
|---|---|---|---|
| hydraulic_pressure | bar | 120 | 210 |
| beam_position_error | mm | -8 | 8 |
| oil_temperature | degC | 35 | 58 |
| cylinder_drift | mm/cycle | 0 | 3 |

Operation outside these limits should trigger investigation. Sustained excursions indicate developing faults and should be escalated per the alerting matrix.

## 3. Common Fault Modes
### 3.1 Hydraulic cylinder drift
Internal leakage causes beam position error and skid marks.

**Typical root causes:** Piston seal wear, Oil contamination, Valve leakage.

### 3.2 Skid button wear
Worn skids cause non-uniform heating and slab marks.

**Typical root causes:** Long service, Overheating, Abrasion.

### 3.3 Oil overheating
High oil temperature degrades viscosity and seals.

**Typical root causes:** Cooler fouling, Low oil level, Continuous high load.

- **Symptom related to Oil overheating:** Check Cooler fouling, Low oil level. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.

### 3.1 Impeller dust build-up / imbalance
Dust accretion on impeller raises vibration and unbalance.

**Typical root causes:** Inadequate gas cleaning, High dust load, Missed cleaning cycle.

### 3.2 Impeller erosion
Abrasive dust thins blades, shifting balance over time.

**Typical root causes:** High particulate, Long service hours, Wrong blade material.

### 3.3 Bearing failure
Bearing temperature and vibration rise sharply before seizure.

**Typical root causes:** Lube starvation, Water ingress, Overheating.

**Asset ID:** SP-IDF-02  
**Plant Area:** Sinter Making  
**Criticality:** High  
**Manufacturer:** Danieli  
**Installed:** 2015

## 1. Overview
The Sinter Plant ID Fan (SP-IDF-02) is a high-criticality asset in the Sinter Making area. This manual describes its monitored parameters, normal operating envelope, common fault modes, recommended maintenance schedule, troubleshooting guidance, and associated spare parts.

## 2. Monitored Parameters & Normal Operating Envelope
| Parameter | Unit | Normal Low | Normal High |
|---|---|---|---|
| vibration | mm/s | 1.0 | 6.0 |
| bearing_temperature | degC | 40 | 80 |
| motor_current | A | 200 | 480 |
| inlet_pressure | mmWC | -1200 | -700 |

Operation outside these limits should trigger investigation. Sustained excursions indicate developing faults and should be escalated per the alerting matrix.

## 3. Common Fault Modes
### 3.1 Impeller dust build-up / imbalance
Dust accretion on impeller raises vibration and unbalance.

**Typical root causes:** Inadequate gas cleaning, High dust load, Missed cleaning cycle.

### 3.2 Impeller erosion
Abrasive dust thins blades, shifting balance over time.

**Typical root causes:** High particulate, Long service hours, Wrong blade material.

### 3.3 Bearing failure
Bearing temperature and vibration rise sharply before seizure.

**Typical root causes:** Lube starvation, Water ingress, Overheating.

## 4. Recommended Maintenance Schedule
| Task | Interval |
|---|---|
| Visual inspection & cleaning | Weekly |
| Lubrication / fluid check | Monthly |
| Vibration & thermography survey | Quarterly |
| Condition-based overhaul | As indicated by trend / RUL |

## 5. Troubleshooting Guide
- **Symptom related to Impeller dust build-up / imbalance:** Check Inadequate gas cleaning, High dust load. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.
- **Symptom related to Impeller erosion:** Check High particulate, Long service hours. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.
- **Symptom related to Bearing failure:** Check Lube starvation, Water ingress. Verify readings against Section 2 limits, inspect affected sub-assembly, and replace worn spares from Section 6 if confirmed.

## 6. Associated Spare Parts
- Fan impeller
- Plummer block bearing
- Wear liner
- Coupling

## 7. Safety Notes
Always apply Lockout-Tagout (LOTO) before intervention. Confirm zero energy state. Refer to the relevant SOP before performing any maintenance task on this asset.

# SOP — Fault Response Procedure: Blast Furnace Stove (BFS-01)

**Applies to:** BFS-01 | **Area:** Iron Making | **Criticality:** Critical

## Purpose
This SOP defines the approved steps for the fault response procedure of the Blast Furnace Stove to ensure personnel safety, equipment integrity, and process continuity.

## Pre-requisites
- Valid work permit and PPE (helmet, gloves, safety shoes, hearing protection).
- Communication established with control room.
- Relevant isolation points identified.

## Procedure
1. On alarm, record the active parameter excursion against Section 2 limits.
2. Reduce load / transfer duty to standby unit if available.
3. Inspect for the most probable fault mode (e.g., Checker brick degradation).
4. Isolate per the LOTO SOP before any physical intervention.
5. Diagnose using the troubleshooting guide in the equipment manual.
6. Replace confirmed worn spares; record part numbers consumed.
7. Re-commission per the start-up SOP and monitor trend for 24 h.

## Records
Log all actions, readings, and consumed spares in the digital maintenance logbook.

# SOP — Lockout-Tagout (LOTO) Procedure: Blast Furnace Stove (BFS-01)

**Applies to:** BFS-01 | **Area:** Iron Making | **Criticality:** Critical

## Purpose
This SOP defines the approved steps for the lockout-tagout (loto) procedure of the Blast Furnace Stove to ensure personnel safety, equipment integrity, and process continuity.

## Pre-requisites
- Valid work permit and PPE (helmet, gloves, safety shoes, hearing protection).
- Communication established with control room.
- Relevant isolation points identified.

## Procedure
1. Notify control room and obtain permit-to-work.
2. Identify all energy sources (electrical, hydraulic, pneumatic, thermal).
3. Switch off and isolate the main drive at the local isolator.
4. Apply personal locks and tags at each isolation point.
5. Dissipate stored energy (bleed hydraulic/pneumatic pressure, allow thermal cool-down).
6. Verify zero-energy state using approved test method.
7. Proceed with maintenance only after verification.

## Records
Log all actions, readings, and consumed spares in the digital maintenance logbook.

# SOP — Controlled Shutdown Procedure: Blast Furnace Stove (BFS-01)

**Applies to:** BFS-01 | **Area:** Iron Making | **Criticality:** Critical

## Purpose
This SOP defines the approved steps for the controlled shutdown procedure of the Blast Furnace Stove to ensure personnel safety, equipment integrity, and process continuity.

## Pre-requisites
- Valid work permit and PPE (helmet, gloves, safety shoes, hearing protection).
- Communication established with control room.
- Relevant isolation points identified.

## Procedure
1. Obtain control-room clearance to stop.
2. Reduce load progressively to minimum.
3. Stop the main drive.
4. Keep auxiliaries running for cool-down as specified.
5. Isolate if maintenance is to follow (see LOTO SOP).

## Records
Log all actions, readings, and consumed spares in the digital maintenance logbook.

# SOP — Safe Start-up Procedure: Blast Furnace Stove (BFS-01)

**Applies to:** BFS-01 | **Area:** Iron Making | **Criticality:** Critical

## Purpose
This SOP defines the approved steps for the safe start-up procedure of the Blast Furnace Stove to ensure personnel safety, equipment integrity, and process continuity.

## Pre-requisites
- Valid work permit and PPE (helmet, gloves, safety shoes, hearing protection).
- Communication established with control room.
- Relevant isolation points identified.

## Procedure
1. Confirm all maintenance locks/tags removed and area clear.
2. Verify lubrication levels and cooling media availability.
3. Energise auxiliaries (lube, cooling) and confirm healthy readings.
4. Start main drive at minimum load.
5. Ramp to operating point while watching Section 2 parameters.
6. Hand over to operations once stable.

## Records
Log all actions, readings, and consumed spares in the digital maintenance logbook.

# SOP — Fault Response Procedure: BOF Lance Hoist (BOF-LH-03)

**Applies to:** BOF-LH-03 | **Area:** Steel Making | **Criticality:** Critical

## Purpose
This SOP defines the approved steps for the fault response procedure of the BOF Lance Hoist to ensure personnel safety, equipment integrity, and process continuity.

## Pre-requisites
- Valid work permit and PPE (helmet, gloves, safety shoes, hearing protection).
- Communication established with control room.
- Relevant isolation points identified.

1. On alarm, record the active parameter excursion against Section 2 limits.

## Records

# SOP — Lockout-Tagout (LOTO) Procedure: BOF Lance Hoist (BOF-LH-03)

**Applies to:** BOF-LH-03 | **Area:** Steel Making | **Criticality:** Critical

## Purpose
This SOP defines the approved steps for the lockout-tagout (loto) procedure of the BOF Lance Hoist to ensure personnel safety, equipment integrity, and process continuity.

## Pre-requisites
- Valid work permit and PPE (helmet, gloves, safety shoes, hearing protection).
- Communication established with control room.
- Relevant isolation points identified.

## Procedure
1. Notify control room and obtain permit-to-work.
2. Identify all energy sources (electrical, hydraulic, pneumatic, thermal).
3. Switch off and isolate the main drive at the local isolator.
4. Apply personal locks and tags at each isolation point.
5. Dissipate stored energy (bleed hydraulic/pneumatic pressure, allow thermal cool-down).
6. Verify zero-energy state using approved test method.
7. Proceed with maintenance only after verification.

## Records
Log all actions, readings, and consumed spares in the digital maintenance logbook.

# SOP — Lockout-Tagout (LOTO) Procedure: Coke Oven Pushing Ram (COR-02)

**Applies to:** COR-02 | **Area:** Coke Making | **Criticality:** High

## Purpose
This SOP defines the approved steps for the lockout-tagout (loto) procedure of the Coke Oven Pushing Ram to ensure personnel safety, equipment integrity, and process continuity.

## Pre-requisites
- Valid work permit and PPE (helmet, gloves, safety shoes, hearing protection).
- Communication established with control room.
- Relevant isolation points identified.

## Procedure
1. Notify control room and obtain permit-to-work.
2. Identify all energy sources (electrical, hydraulic, pneumatic, thermal).
3. Switch off and isolate the main drive at the local isolator.
4. Apply personal locks and tags at each isolation point.
5. Dissipate stored energy (bleed hydraulic/pneumatic pressure, allow thermal cool-down).
6. Verify zero-energy state using approved test method.
7. Proceed with maintenance only after verification.

## Records
Log all actions, readings, and consumed spares in the digital maintenance logbook.

# SOP — Fault Response Procedure: Coke Oven Pushing Ram (COR-03)

**Applies to:** COR-03 | **Area:** Coke Making | **Criticality:** High

## Purpose
This SOP defines the approved steps for the fault response procedure of the Coke Oven Pushing Ram to ensure personnel safety, equipment integrity, and process continuity.

## Pre-requisites
- Valid work permit and PPE (helmet, gloves, safety shoes, hearing protection).
- Communication established with control room.
- Relevant isolation points identified.

## Procedure
1. On alarm, record the active parameter excursion against Section 2 limits.
2. Reduce load / transfer duty to standby unit if available.
3. Inspect for the most probable fault mode (e.g., Hard pushing / sticky oven).
4. Isolate per the LOTO SOP before any physical intervention.
5. Diagnose using the troubleshooting guide in the equipment manual.
6. Replace confirmed worn spares; record part numbers consumed.
7. Re-commission per the start-up SOP and monitor trend for 24 h.

## Records
Log all actions, readings, and consumed spares in the digital maintenance logbook.

3. Inspect for the most probable fault mode (e.g., Cavitation).
4. Isolate per the LOTO SOP before any physical intervention.
5. Diagnose using the troubleshooting guide in the equipment manual.
6. Replace confirmed worn spares; record part numbers consumed.
7. Re-commission per the start-up SOP and monitor trend for 24 h.

## Records
Log all actions, readings, and consumed spares in the digital maintenance logbook.

# SOP — Lockout-Tagout (LOTO) Procedure: Cooling Water Pump (CWP-07)

**Applies to:** CWP-07 | **Area:** Utilities | **Criticality:** Medium

## Purpose
This SOP defines the approved steps for the lockout-tagout (loto) procedure of the Cooling Water Pump to ensure personnel safety, equipment integrity, and process continuity.

## Pre-requisites
- Valid work permit and PPE (helmet, gloves, safety shoes, hearing protection).
- Communication established with control room.
- Relevant isolation points identified.

## Procedure
1. Notify control room and obtain permit-to-work.
2. Identify all energy sources (electrical, hydraulic, pneumatic, thermal).
3. Switch off and isolate the main drive at the local isolator.
4. Apply personal locks and tags at each isolation point.
5. Dissipate stored energy (bleed hydraulic/pneumatic pressure, allow thermal cool-down).
6. Verify zero-energy state using approved test method.
7. Proceed with maintenance only after verification.

## Records
Log all actions, readings, and consumed spares in the digital maintenance logbook.

- Communication established with control room.
- Relevant isolation points identified.

## Procedure
1. Confirm all maintenance locks/tags removed and area clear.
2. Verify lubrication levels and cooling media availability.
3. Energise auxiliaries (lube, cooling) and confirm healthy readings.
4. Start main drive at minimum load.
5. Ramp to operating point while watching Section 2 parameters.
6. Hand over to operations once stable.

## Records
Log all actions, readings, and consumed spares in the digital maintenance logbook.

# SOP — Fault Response Procedure: Reheating Furnace Walking Beam Drive (RHF-WBD-01)

**Applies to:** RHF-WBD-01 | **Area:** Hot Rolling | **Criticality:** High

## Purpose
This SOP defines the approved steps for the fault response procedure of the Reheating Furnace Walking Beam Drive to ensure personnel safety, equipment integrity, and process continuity.

## Pre-requisites
- Valid work permit and PPE (helmet, gloves, safety shoes, hearing protection).
- Communication established with control room.
- Relevant isolation points identified.

## Procedure
1. On alarm, record the active parameter excursion against Section 2 limits.
2. Reduce load / transfer duty to standby unit if available.
3. Inspect for the most probable fault mode (e.g., Hydraulic cylinder drift).
4. Isolate per the LOTO SOP before any physical intervention.
5. Diagnose using the troubleshooting guide in the equipment manual.
6. Replace confirmed worn spares; record part numbers consumed.
7. Re-commission per the start-up SOP and monitor trend for 24 h.

## Records
Log all actions, readings, and consumed spares in the digital maintenance logbook.

# SOP — Lockout-Tagout (LOTO) Procedure: Reheating Furnace Walking Beam Drive (RHF-WBD-01)

**Applies to:** RHF-WBD-01 | **Area:** Hot Rolling | **Criticality:** High

## Purpose
This SOP defines the approved steps for the lockout-tagout (loto) procedure of the Reheating Furnace Walking Beam Drive to ensure personnel safety, equipment integrity, and process continuity.

## Pre-requisites
- Valid work permit and PPE (helmet, gloves, safety shoes, hearing protection).
- Communication established with control room.
- Relevant isolation points identified.

## Procedure
1. Notify control room and obtain permit-to-work.
2. Identify all energy sources (electrical, hydraulic, pneumatic, thermal).
3. Switch off and isolate the main drive at the local isolator.
4. Apply personal locks and tags at each isolation point.
5. Dissipate stored energy (bleed hydraulic/pneumatic pressure, allow thermal cool-down).
6. Verify zero-energy state using approved test method.
7. Proceed with maintenance only after verification.

## Records
Log all actions, readings, and consumed spares in the digital maintenance logbook.

3. Switch off and isolate the main drive at the local isolator.
4. Apply personal locks and tags at each isolation point.
5. Dissipate stored energy (bleed hydraulic/pneumatic pressure, allow thermal cool-down).
6. Verify zero-energy state using approved test method.
7. Proceed with maintenance only after verification.

## Records
Log all actions, readings, and consumed spares in the digital maintenance logbook.

# SOP — Controlled Shutdown Procedure: Sinter Plant ID Fan (SP-IDF-04)

**Applies to:** SP-IDF-04 | **Area:** Sinter Making | **Criticality:** High

## Purpose
This SOP defines the approved steps for the controlled shutdown procedure of the Sinter Plant ID Fan to ensure personnel safety, equipment integrity, and process continuity.

## Pre-requisites
- Valid work permit and PPE (helmet, gloves, safety shoes, hearing protection).
- Communication established with control room.
- Relevant isolation points identified.

## Procedure
1. Obtain control-room clearance to stop.
2. Reduce load progressively to minimum.
3. Stop the main drive.
4. Keep auxiliaries running for cool-down as specified.
5. Isolate if maintenance is to follow (see LOTO SOP).

## Records
Log all actions, readings, and consumed spares in the digital maintenance logbook.

# SOP — Safe Start-up Procedure: Sinter Plant ID Fan (SP-IDF-04)

**Applies to:** SP-IDF-04 | **Area:** Sinter Making | **Criticality:** High

## Purpose
This SOP defines the approved steps for the safe start-up procedure of the Sinter Plant ID Fan to ensure personnel safety, equipment integrity, and process continuity.

## Pre-requisites
- Valid work permit and PPE (helmet, gloves, safety shoes, hearing protection).
- Communication established with control room.
- Relevant isolation points identified.

## Procedure
1. Confirm all maintenance locks/tags removed and area clear.
2. Verify lubrication levels and cooling media availability.
3. Energise auxiliaries (lube, cooling) and confirm healthy readings.
4. Start main drive at minimum load.
5. Ramp to operating point while watching Section 2 parameters.
6. Hand over to operations once stable.

## Records
Log all actions, readings, and consumed spares in the digital maintenance logbook.

# Failure Analysis Report — BFS-01 — FR-20250124-001

**Asset:** BFS-01 (Blast Furnace Stove)  
**Area:** Iron Making  
**Date of failure:** 2025-01-24  
**Downtime:** 18 hours  
**Fault mode:** Hot blast valve leakage

## Event Description
Operations reported an abnormal condition on BFS-01. Worn valve seat allows hot blast to bypass, lowering blast temperature. The parameter 'waste_gas_temp' deviated from its normal band (250–400 degC) prior to the trip.

## Root Cause
Investigation concluded the primary root cause was **inadequate cooling water**, leading to hot blast valve leakage.

## Corrective Action Taken
- Isolated the asset per LOTO SOP.
- Replaced affected component (Cooling stave).
- Restored normal operating parameters and monitored for 24 hours.

## Preventive Recommendation
- Tighten inspection interval for the affected sub-assembly.
- Add 'waste_gas_temp' to the early-warning watch list with alert at 380 degC.
- Ensure spare 'Cooling stave' is held in stock given lead time.

# Failure Analysis Report — SP-IDF-03 — FR-20250221-003

**Asset:** SP-IDF-03 (Sinter Plant ID Fan)  
**Area:** Sinter Making  
**Date of failure:** 2025-02-21  
**Downtime:** 6 hours  
**Fault mode:** Impeller dust build-up / imbalance

## Event Description
Operations reported an abnormal condition on SP-IDF-03. Dust accretion on impeller raises vibration and unbalance. The parameter 'bearing_temperature' deviated from its normal band (40–80 degC) prior to the trip.

## Root Cause
Investigation concluded the primary root cause was **missed cleaning cycle**, leading to impeller dust build-up / imbalance.

## Corrective Action Taken
- Isolated the asset per LOTO SOP.
- Replaced affected component (Plummer block bearing).
- Restored normal operating parameters and monitored for 24 hours.

## Preventive Recommendation
- Tighten inspection interval for the affected sub-assembly.
- Add 'bearing_temperature' to the early-warning watch list with alert at 76 degC.
- Ensure spare 'Plummer block bearing' is held in stock given lead time.
